index.title: Resilience — Performance Physical Therapy in Austin
index.description: Performance Physical Therapy in Austin that makes you strong and durable, not just pain-free. Build a body that lasts.

blocks.eyebrow: Performance Physical Therapy for Active Lives in Austin
blocks.headline: Build a body that lasts
blocks.subhead: We fix the root cause of your pain and build the strength underneath it — so you come back tougher than the injury that stopped you.

blocks.headline: Pain-free isn't the finish line
blocks.body: Resting until it stops hurting only sets you up to break down again. Real recovery means rebuilding the strength and control that keep pain from coming back — so you can train, run, lift, and live without holding back.

blocks.headline: We care about your comeback as much as you do
blocks.tagline: We train hard too, and we know how it feels when your body won't let you.

steps.title: Book a FREE phone consult
steps.body: Spend 15 minutes with us so we can understand your goals, your injury, and whether we're the right fit.

steps.title: Get matched with your therapist
steps.body: We pair you with the clinician who specializes in exactly what you need and build a plan around your life.

steps.title: Come back stronger and durable
steps.body: Execute the plan with a coach in your corner and return more resilient than you were before the injury.

blocks.headline: A body that breaks down keeps you on the sidelines
blocks.body: Performance Physical Therapy doesn't just quiet the pain — it eliminates the cause and builds the durability that keeps you in the game for the long haul.

items.title: Performance Physical Therapy
items.body: We don't slap a band-aid on your pain — we find why it started and fix it. Every session is 60 minutes, one-on-one, combining hands-on care, dry needling, and real strength work.

items.title: Evidence-Based Care
items.body: Movement assessments and objective testing drive every plan, so we measure progress instead of guessing at it.

items.title: Strength Programming
items.body: Your fix has to hold under load. We build progressive strength so your results last well beyond the clinic.

blocks.headline: We've helped active Austinites build bodies that last
blocks.body: It starts with a free consult and a plan built around your goals. From there you execute with us in your corner — clear next steps after every session, a program you can actually follow, and direct access to your clinician.
items: Get stronger and more durable than before

blocks.headline: See what we're all about
blocks.body: A clinic built for performance and a team that treats your goals like their own. Let's find out if we're the right fit.

physical-therapy.title: Physical Therapy — Resilience
physical-therapy.description: Performance Physical Therapy in Austin: 60-minute one-on-one sessions that fix the root cause and build lasting strength.

blocks.headline: We fix the root cause and build the strength to keep it gone
blocks.subhead: Every session is 60 minutes, one-on-one with your therapist. No double-booking, no handing you off to an aide.

blocks.headline: What makes Performance PT different
body: Most clinics treat the spot that hurts and send you on your way. We look at how your whole body moves, find why the pain started, and build the strength and mechanics that keep it from coming back.
That means more time with you, sharper assessments, and a plan built around the active life you actually want to live.

items.title: Hands-on manual therapy
items.body: Targeted soft-tissue and joint work to restore mobility and calm painful tissue quickly.

items.title: Dry needling
items.body: Precise relief for stubborn muscular pain and trigger points that won't let go.

items.title: Strength & performance
items.body: Real gym-based work that rebuilds capacity so your fix holds under load.

items.title: Movement assessments
items.body: A full-body look at how you move to find the true driver of your pain.

items.title: Strength programming
items.body: A progressive plan you can actually follow, built around your goals and schedule.

items.title: Return-to-sport testing
items.body: Objective benchmarks so you come back when you're truly ready — not just when it stops hurting.

steps.title: Assess
steps.body: A full-body evaluation to find the true source of your pain — not just where you feel it.

steps.title: Treat
steps.body: Hands-on care plus a personalized plan you can actually fit into your week.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: Progressive strength work so you come back more durable than you were before.

blocks.headline: Conditions we treat every day
blocks.body: If pain is keeping you from the activities you love, there's a good chance we can help. A few of the most common reasons people come to Resilience:
items: Running injuries (runner's knee, IT band, shin splints)
Staying strong and active through your 50s, 60s, and beyond

blocks.headline: Not sure if we're the right fit?
blocks.body: That's exactly what the free 15-minute call is for. No pressure — just answers.

about.title: About Us — Resilience
about.description: Meet the Resilience team — performance physical therapists in Austin who train hard too.

blocks.headline: We train hard too
blocks.subhead: We're lifters, runners, and athletes first — and we know exactly how it feels when an injury takes that away.

blocks.headline: Our story
body: Resilience started with a simple frustration: traditional physical therapy wasn't built for people who push their bodies. Fifteen-minute visits, hot packs, and a generic exercise sheet don't cut it when your goal is to stay strong for the long haul.
So Michael Wilson built something different — a clinic where every session is an hour, one-on-one, focused on making you strong and durable, not just pain-free on paper.

items.title: Root cause, always
items.body: We never settle for masking symptoms. We find why it started and fix it for good.

items.title: Strength is the medicine
items.body: Our goal isn't just zero pain — it's a body resilient enough to handle whatever you ask of it.

items.title: You're an athlete
items.body: However you move, we treat you like the athlete you are and train you like one.

items.title: Michael Wilson
items.body: Founder & Performance PT. Leads every new client's first consultation.

items.body: Specializes in strength-based rehab and getting you back under load safely.

blog.title: Blog — Resilience
blog.description: Tips, guides, and stories from the Resilience Physical Therapy team in Austin.

blocks.headline: Get strong, stay durable
blocks.subhead: Practical guidance from our team on training, recovery, and building a body that lasts.

posts.title: Why strength is the most underrated form of rehab
posts.excerpt: Rest and stretching feel productive, but they rarely make you durable. Here's why loading the right way is what actually keeps pain gone.
body: When something hurts, the instinct is to back off and wait for it to settle. That can take the edge off in the short term — but it does nothing to fix why the pain showed up in the first place.
Tissue adapts to the demands you place on it. When you build strength through a controlled, progressive plan, you give your joints and muscles the capacity to handle real life without breaking down.
That's the whole idea behind Performance PT: we don't just calm the pain down, we build you back up so it stays gone. Strength is the medicine that makes your results last.

posts.title: Coming back from runner's knee without losing your season
posts.excerpt: Runner's knee doesn't have to mean months off. Here's how we get Austin runners back on the road — smartly.
body: Runner's knee is one of the most common reasons runners come to see us — and one of the most fixable.

The key is to address the mechanics and strength gaps driving the pain rather than just resting until it quiets down. Rest alone tends to set you up for the same problem the moment mileage climbs again.
With smart load management, hip and quad strength work, and small gait tweaks, most runners are back to training in weeks, not months.

posts.title: Staying strong and active well past 50
posts.excerpt: Slowing down isn't inevitable. Here's how the right training keeps you moving, lifting, and living on your terms for decades.
body: The aches and stiffness people chalk up to 'getting older' are often less about age and more about losing strength and capacity over time.
The good news: strength is trainable at every age. With the right plan, you can keep climbing stairs, lifting grandkids, hiking, and doing the things you love without fear of injury.
We build programs around what matters to you — so staying active isn't something you give up, it's something you keep getting better at.
